primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation du critère SPPI (« prêt basique ») pour un actif financier comportant des clauses contractuelles pouvant modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie, notamment en cas d’événement déclencheur

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping financial instruments classification and cash flow characteristics issues, so IFRS 9 is the single governing lineage for the SPPI assessment and IAS 39 is discarded from the candidate set.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is directly about SPPI and subsequent classification of financial assets, which is explicitly governed by IFRS 9.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether contractual cash flows, including trigger-based changes in timing or amount, can still be SPPI and therefore qualify for non-FVTPL classification.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify the meaning of a basic lending arrangement, interest, leverage, and modified time value of money in the SPPI analysis.
These paragraphs provide examples and limiting guidance on prepayment, extension, contingent features, and de minimis or non-genuine features relevant to triggered cash flow changes.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs relate to insurance contract cash flow measurement and discounting, not IFRS 9 SPPI classification of financial assets.
These paragraphs relate to inventories and financing elements in inventory purchases, not SPPI assessment under IFRS 9.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest within a hold-to-collect business model
Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest within a hold-to-collect-and-sell business model
Financial assets that do not meet the sole payments of principal and interest condition or otherwise fall outside the amortised cost and fair value through other comprehensive income models

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats amortised cost as a distinct subsequent measurement model when SPPI is met and the business model is hold to collect.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats fair value through other comprehensive income as a distinct subsequent measurement model when SPPI is met and the business model is achieved by collecting cash flows and selling.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats fair value through profit or loss as the residual distinct measurement model when the asset does not qualify for the SPPI-based categories.
